    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go", "symbol": "CheckPermissions"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go", "symbol": "GetPermissions"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go", "symbol": "GetChildren"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/ldap/ldap_groups.go", "symbol": "GetTeamGroupsForUser"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana organization/team/user permission hierarchy is implemented across multiple services and layers:\n\n## Core Models and Services\n\n1. **Organization User Role Assignment**: `pkg/services/org/model.go` defines the `OrgUser` struct with a `RoleType` field that determines user's organization-level role (Viewer, Editor, Admin). The `pkg/services/org/org.go` service interface defines methods `AddOrgUser`, `UpdateOrgUser`, and `RemoveOrgUser` for managing org membership and roles.\n\n2. **Team Model and Membership**: `pkg/services/team/model.go` defines `Team` and `TeamMember` models. `TeamMember` tracks team membership with an `External` boolean flag to mark LDAP/OAuth synced members, and a `Permission` field for team-level admin/member roles. The team service in `pkg/services/team/team.go` provides methods like `CreateTeam`, `GetTeamMembers`, and membership management.\n\n## Team Group Synchronization\n\n3. **Team Group Sync**: The codebase implements external group synchronization through:\n   - `pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go` handles team member management with support for externally synced members\n   - `pkg/services/ldap/ldap_groups.go` implements LDAP group synchronization for teams, extracting group memberships from LDAP\n   - `pkg/services/ldap/model.go` defines `LDAPGroupToGrafanaTeamMap` for mapping LDAP groups to Grafana teams\n   - `pkg/services/login/model.go` defines `ExternalUserInfo` with `Groups` and `SkipTeamSync` fields for OAuth/SAML team provisioning\n   - `pkg/services/ldap/service/ldap_service.go` handles active LDAP synchronization of team memberships\n\n## RBAC Fine-Grained Permission Evaluation\n\n4. **Permission Evaluation Architecture**: The RBAC evaluation chain involves:\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines the core `AccessControl` interface with `Evaluate()` method\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/accesscontrol.go` implements permission evaluation by checking user permissions against evaluators\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` implements `Evaluator` interface that builds permission checks from actions and scopes\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines `Permission`, `Role`, and `RoleDTO` models with action/scope matching\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go` is the gRPC-based authz service implementing fine-grained RBAC with permission caching\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/mapper.go` maps permissions from the RBAC store to evaluatable permission sets\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/resolver.go` resolves scope attributes (like folder UIDs) in permission evaluation\n\n5. **RBAC Storage and Queries**: \n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/store.go` provides the main store interface\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/permission_store.go` handles permission queries\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/folder_store.go` manages folder-specific permissions\n   - SQL query templates (`permission_query.sql`, `folder_query.sql`, `basic_role_query.sql`) implement the actual permission evaluation logic\n\n## Folder Permission Inheritance\n\n6. **Folder Permissions Service**: \n   - `pkg/services/folder/model.go` defines the `Folder` model\n   - `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go` and `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder_unifiedstorage.go` implement folder service with permission checking\n   - `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go` has `getRootFolders()` and `getAvailableNonRootFolders()` that filter folders based on user permissions from the evaluator\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides `FolderPermissionsService` that wraps resource permissions\n\n7. **Resource Permissions Service**:\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` implements `GetPermissions()` to retrieve resource-level permissions\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go` queries the database for user/team/role permissions on resources\n   - This service supports permissions at folder, dashboard, and team levels\n\n## API Handlers and Integration\n\n8. **API Handlers**:\n   - `pkg/api/org_users.go` implements REST endpoints for org user management (add, update, remove org users)\n   - `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` implements folder permission endpoints\n   - `pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` implements dashboard permission endpoints\n   - `pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go` implements team membership endpoints with permission enforcement\n\n9. **IAM Registry**: \n   - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/register.go` registers IAM API handlers including team, user, and resource permission handlers\n   - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/team/storage.go` implements K8s-style team storage for the API server\n   - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/resourcepermission/models.go` defines resource permission objects for the API\n\n10. **Middleware and Authorization**:\n    - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` wraps HTTP requests and enforces permission checks\n    - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` maps Kubernetes API verbs to RBAC actions and scopes\n    - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/authorizer.go` authorizes API requests based on RBAC permissions\n\nThe complete hierarchy flows from: OrgUser role assignments → Team membership with external sync → Permission evaluation through RBAC evaluators → Folder/Dashboard permission inheritance, with each layer delegating to lower layers for actual permission checks and scope resolution."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Organization and Team Permission Hierarchy

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement the organization/team/user permission hierarchy: the OrgUser role assignment, the team group sync, the folder permission inheritance, and the RBAC fine-grained permission evaluation.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the org domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement the organization/team/user permission hierarchy. Let me start by exploring the codebase.
Let me continue searching for more specific files related to RBAC and access control:
Now let me compile all my findings into the answer file:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the Grafana codebase and created the answer file at `/workspace/answer.json`.

## Summary

I've identified **54 Go source files** in grafana/grafana that implement the organization/team/user permission hierarchy with four main components:

### Key Findings:

1. **OrgUser Role Assignment** (`pkg/services/org/`):
   - Models define `OrgUser` with `RoleType` (Viewer, Editor, Admin)
   - Service manages AddOrgUser, UpdateOrgUser, RemoveOrgUser operations

2. **Team Group Synchronization** (`pkg/services/team/` and `pkg/services/ldap/`):
   - Team models track `TeamMember` with external flag for LDAP/OAuth synced users
   - LDAP groups service extracts and maps group memberships to teams
   - Support for OAuth/SAML group mapping through `ExternalUserInfo`

3. **RBAC Fine-Grained Permission Evaluation** (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/` and `pkg/services/authz/rbac/`):
   - AccessControl interface evaluates permissions against Evaluators
   - RBAC service implements caching and permission resolution
   - Evaluators combine actions and scopes for granular checks

4. **Folder Permission Inheritance** (`pkg/services/folder/` and resource permissions):
   - Folder service filters results based on user permissions
   - ResourcePermissionsService handles folder/dashboard/team-level permissions
   - FolderPermissionsService inherits and applies permissions

The answer file includes detailed documentation of the call chain and integration points showing how these components work together to implement the complete permission hierarchy.

arguments.query: repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec$ external group sync team ldap oauth saml

arguments.description: Find team group sync implementation files
arguments.prompt:
Search the grafana/grafana codebase for files related to team group synchronization (external group sync, team groups, LDAP group sync). Look for:
1. Files with "group" in the name in team-related paths
2. API handlers for teams/:teamId/groups endpoints
3. Database or store implementations for team groups
4. LDAP team synchronization code

Focus on finding the exact file paths that implement the team group sync feature.

  "text": "The Grafana organization/team/user permission hierarchy is implemented across multiple services and layers:\n\n## Core Models and Services\n\n1. **Organization User Role Assignment**: `pkg/services/org/model.go` defines the `OrgUser` struct with a `RoleType` field that determines user's organization-level role (Viewer, Editor, Admin). The `pkg/services/org/org.go` service interface defines methods `AddOrgUser`, `UpdateOrgUser`, and `RemoveOrgUser` for managing org membership and roles.\n\n2. **Team Model and Membership**: `pkg/services/team/model.go` defines `Team` and `TeamMember` models. `TeamMember` tracks team membership with an `External` boolean flag to mark LDAP/OAuth synced members, and a `Permission` field for team-level admin/member roles. The team service in `pkg/services/team/team.go` provides methods like `CreateTeam`, `GetTeamMembers`, and membership management.\n\n## Team Group Synchronization\n\n3. **Team Group Sync**: The codebase implements external group synchronization through:\n   - `pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go` handles team member management with support for externally synced members\n   - `pkg/services/ldap/ldap_groups.go` implements LDAP group synchronization for teams, extracting group memberships from LDAP\n   - `pkg/services/ldap/model.go` defines `LDAPGroupToGrafanaTeamMap` for mapping LDAP groups to Grafana teams\n   - `pkg/services/login/model.go` defines `ExternalUserInfo` with `Groups` and `SkipTeamSync` fields for OAuth/SAML team provisioning\n   - `pkg/services/ldap/service/ldap_service.go` handles active LDAP synchronization of team memberships\n\n## RBAC Fine-Grained Permission Evaluation\n\n4. **Permission Evaluation Architecture**: The RBAC evaluation chain involves:\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines the core `AccessControl` interface with `Evaluate()` method\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/accesscontrol.go` implements permission evaluation by checking user permissions against evaluators\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` implements `Evaluator` interface that builds permission checks from actions and scopes\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines `Permission`, `Role`, and `RoleDTO` models with action/scope matching\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go` is the gRPC-based authz service implementing fine-grained RBAC with permission caching\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/mapper.go` maps permissions from the RBAC store to evaluatable permission sets\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/resolver.go` resolves scope attributes (like folder UIDs) in permission evaluation\n\n5. **RBAC Storage and Queries**: \n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/store.go` provides the main store interface\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/permission_store.go` handles permission queries\n   - `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/folder_store.go` manages folder-specific permissions\n   - SQL query templates (`permission_query.sql`, `folder_query.sql`, `basic_role_query.sql`) implement the actual permission evaluation logic\n\n## Folder Permission Inheritance\n\n6. **Folder Permissions Service**: \n   - `pkg/services/folder/model.go` defines the `Folder` model\n   - `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go` and `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder_unifiedstorage.go` implement folder service with permission checking\n   - `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go` has `getRootFolders()` and `getAvailableNonRootFolders()` that filter folders based on user permissions from the evaluator\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides `FolderPermissionsService` that wraps resource permissions\n\n7. **Resource Permissions Service**:\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` implements `GetPermissions()` to retrieve resource-level permissions\n   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go` queries the database for user/team/role permissions on resources\n   - This service supports permissions at folder, dashboard, and team levels\n\n## API Handlers and Integration\n\n8. **API Handlers**:\n   - `pkg/api/org_users.go` implements REST endpoints for org user management (add, update, remove org users)\n   - `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` implements folder permission endpoints\n   - `pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` implements dashboard permission endpoints\n   - `pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go` implements team membership endpoints with permission enforcement\n\n9. **IAM Registry**: \n   - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/register.go` registers IAM API handlers including team, user, and resource permission handlers\n   - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/team/storage.go` implements K8s-style team storage for the API server\n   - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/resourcepermission/models.go` defines resource permission objects for the API\n\n10. **Middleware and Authorization**:\n    - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` wraps HTTP requests and enforces permission checks\n    - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` maps Kubernetes API verbs to RBAC actions and scopes\n    - `pkg/registry/apis/iam/authorizer.go` authorizes API requests based on RBAC permissions\n\nThe complete hierarchy flows from: OrgUser role assignments → Team membership with external sync → Permission evaluation through RBAC evaluators → Folder/Dashboard permission inheritance, with each layer delegating to lower layers for actual permission checks and scope resolution."
}
